ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not cover branded instant noodle products; closest parent would be a generic instant noodle food product node.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: Specific branded Korean stir-style instant noodle not available in FoodEx2; A0012 (Noodle products) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this branded instant noodle product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One full packet of Samyang Buldak 2x Spicy Hot Chicken Ramen, prepared with the sauce sachet and garnish, typically weighs 140g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Ramen noodles, cooked (closest approved analogue); primary label data from Samyang Foods Singapore-sold import product NIP cross-referenced against USDA Branded Food entries for Samyang Buldak instant noodle products
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macro and micronutrient values are sourced from the Samyang Buldak 2x Spicy product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as sold in Singapore, cross-referenced and validated against USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entries for Samyang Buldak instant noodle products (USDA Branded Foods database contains Samyang Buldak entries). USDA FoodData Central is the approved secondary global source used here as the primary approved-source anchor for this branded Korean packaged food. Values are consistent with USDA Branded Food records for this product line.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of instant noodles — analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed GI data for wheat-based instant noodles; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care / Brand-Miller et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Samyang Buldak 2x Spicy in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~50 is estimated based on the closest analogous food: wheat-based instant noodles (GI range 40–55) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Diabetes Care 31:12), which is a peer-reviewed source. Glycemic load per 140g serving calculated as (50 × 82g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 41.0. This is classified as low GI per University of Sydney thresholds (GI ≤55 = low), though the GL of 41 per full packet is very high.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — 1,800mg per packet (90% of 2,000mg/day recommended limit)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product, as one full packet delivers 1,800mg sodium — 90% of the daily recommended limit — and unlike soup-style noodles, the stir-style format means all sodium in the sauce is consumed with no broth to discard.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per serving (GL 41) despite moderate GI (~50); 82g carbohydrate per packet
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI is approximately 50 (low-medium), the glycemic load of 41 per full 140g packet is very high due to the large carbohydrate mass (82g), making this product unsuitable as a regular meal for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without careful portion control and carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1,800mg per packet) and saturated fat (6.0g per packet); ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of very high sodium (1,800mg per packet), saturated fat (6.0g per packet), and ultra-processed ingredient profile makes this product a high-concern food for individuals with cardiovascular disease, who should limit consumption to occasional and small portions only.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Extreme capsaicin load (~10,000 SHU) and very high sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The extreme capsaicin load (~10,000 SHU) may activate TRPV1 receptors upregulated in inflamed synovial tissue, potentially worsening joint pain and increasing intestinal permeability, while the very high sodium content can exacerbate systemic inflammation in individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

gerd.guidance: The extreme capsaicin content (~10,000 SHU) is a potent gastric irritant that can trigger or significantly worsen GERD symptoms, making this product highly inadvisable for individuals with gastroesophageal reflux disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Samyang Buldak 2x Spicy Hot Chicken Ramen (불닭볶음면 2배 매운맛) is a Korean ultra-processed instant stir noodle manufactured by Samyang Foods Co., Ltd. (South Korea) and widely sold in Singapore supermarkets and Korean grocery stores. It gained global viral fame through the 'Fire Noodle Challenge' on YouTube (from 2014) and subsequent TikTok iterations, making it one of the most recognisable Korean instant noodle products in Singapore alongside Shin Ramyun. The 2x Spicy variant (also known as Haek Buldak) is rated at approximately 10,000 Scoville Heat Units (SHU) — roughly 5× the heat of Tabasco sauce — making it genuinely extremely hot by commercial food standards. Preparation involves boiling the noodles, draining, and stir-frying with the liquid sauce sachet and a dry garnish sachet containing sesame seeds and seaweed flakes. A fried egg on top is a near-universal Singapore preparation, as the fat in the egg yolk reduces capsaicin mucosal contact time. Nutritionally, one 140g packet provides approximately 529 kcal, 17g protein, 16g fat, and 82g carbohydrate, with a very high sodium content of 1,800mg — 90% of the recommended daily limit in a single meal. Because this is a stir-style noodle, all sodium in the sauce is consumed (no broth to discard). The glycemic index is estimated at ~50 (low-medium) but the glycemic load per packet is very high at 41. Clinically, this product is of high concern for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, GERD, and rheumatoid arthritis due to its extreme sodium, capsaicin, and carbohydrate load.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient data is directly from the product's Nutrition Information Panel as sold in Singapore, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Samyang Buldak products. GI is an analogous estimate from a peer-reviewed source (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) via the University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented as an estimate.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data are not reported on the standard product NIP and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product as of April 2026. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 as confirmed by product label. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as confirmed by product label. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 as confirmed by product label. The extreme capsaicin load (~10,000 SHU) is a critical clinical factor beyond standard nutritional analysis, particularly relevant for GERD, IBS, and rheumatoid arthritis patients. Nutrient source: values from Samyang Foods Singapore-sold import product NIP, validated against USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (approved source: USDA Branded Food). GI source: analogous estimate based on Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Diabetes Care 31:12), accessed via University of Sydney GI Database framework.
